humanSummary.text: Initial audits found P1 blockers in runtime report contract validation, whole-report redaction, screenshot provenance, UUID7 parity, and local path detection. Repair commits 5a65cf9, 724ed74, and a63e346 fixed those blockers. The final focused re-audit found no remaining P0/P1/P2 findings; one direct-corpus fixture coverage polish item was recorded as UTSUSHI-142.

findings.title: Direct corpus local-path invalid fixtures are indirect
findings.description: The validator scans corpus JSON directly, but the new on-disk invalid corpus fixtures exercise embedded UNC, unlisted Unix, and Windows-drive cases through referenced report files rather than placing those sensitive strings directly in corpus metadata.

evidence.detail: Final focused re-audit confirmed the P1 redaction bug was fixed and noted direct-corpus invalid fixture coverage as polish.

findings.impact: Future refactors could weaken direct corpus metadata scanning while report-level invalid fixtures still pass.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: The corpus validator rejects embedded UNC, unlisted Unix absolute, and Windows-drive local paths when they appear directly in corpus JSON.
Managed artifact-store URIs, redaction markers, and normal prose with slashes remain accepted.

acceptanceCriteria: The corpus validator rejects embedded UNC, unlisted Unix absolute, and Windows-drive local paths when they appear directly in corpus JSON.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.
